message:
perf(tracking): add batch processing for processed log entries

Optimize session tracking I/O by batching multiple log entries into
single file operations, and improve data integrity by deferring
processed markers until playbook merge succeeds.

## Problem
Previously, each processed session was immediately written to the log
file individually. When processing many sessions (e.g., during bulk
reflect), this caused:
- Excessive file I/O operations (one write per session)
- Risk of partial state if playbook merge failed after marking processed
- Potential file contention in high-throughput scenarios

## Solution

### src/tracking.ts - New appendBatch() method
- Add appendBatch(entries[], options?) method to ProcessedLog class
- Refactor append() to delegate to appendBatch([entry])
- Batch entries are normalized, serialized, and written in a single I/O
- Maintains file locking semantics for thread safety
- Empty batches short-circuit without touching the filesystem

### src/orchestrator.ts - Deferred batch writes
- Replace immediate append() calls with pendingProcessedEntries array
- Accumulate all processed entries during reflection phase
- Only write to log AFTER playbook merge succeeds
- This prevents data loss scenario where:
  1. Session marked as processed
  2. Playbook merge fails
  3. Session is never reprocessed (delta lost forever)

- Add ProcessedEntry to type imports for the pending array
- Write pending entries even when no deltas generated (prevents
  infinite retry of empty sessions)
- Remove obsolete comments about skipLock and incremental saves

## Performance Impact
- Reduces file writes from O(n) to O(1) during bulk operations
- Typical reflect of 10 sessions: 10 writes → 1 write
- Maintains atomicity guarantees via existing file locking

## Data Integrity
- Sessions only marked processed after successful persistence
- Crash/error during merge = sessions remain unprocessed
- Next reflect will reprocess and regenerate deltas
